title: salix novae-angliae
intro: Salix novae-angliae, also known as the New England willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its slender branches and lance-shaped leaves. The medicinal properties of Salix novae-angliae lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in relieving pain and reducing fever. Herbal preparations of Salix novae-angliae can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including headaches, sore throats, and arthritis.
